purpose: Machine-readable canonical source for reviewer-blocker adjudications. Companion to pipeline/audits/reviewer_blocker_adjudications.md. Consumed by pipeline/generate_round_walkthrough.py to populate adjudication status for propagated blockers. The walkthrough generator merges adjudications from this file AND from verdict.meta_review.adjudications, deduping by blocker_summary_hash.

adjudications.blocker_summary_excerpt: Highly likely to be redundant with current standard of care given the profoundly favorable composite z-score and high D2 clinical signal. Without a clear differentiation analysis vs. incretin-class agents for the obesity+T2D+MASH indication, the candidate is a likely 'me-too' position.
adjudications.summary: Out-of-scope clinical positioning per plan.md §1.2; pipeline ranks target-agnostically, downstream class-vs-comparator analysis is a separate workstream. R2 critique was raised in R10 cached verdict.
adjudications.action_taken: N/A (no methodology change; concern acknowledged but out-of-scope for AC-5 acceptance)
adjudications.notes: blocker_summary_hash is null because R10's R2 blocker text was lost when cache was cleared in R11; the adjudication is preserved by persona+round binding for audit trail. Future R2 blockers with the same intent should reference ADJ-001.

adjudications.blocker_summary_excerpt: The candidate exhibits exceptionally poor mechanism differentiation and low modulatability scores, indicating it is likely an intractable or broadly pleiotropic core signaling node.
adjudications.summary: Same architectural rebuttal as ADJ-002: D5/D6 negative scores are by-design surfaced; post-hoc platform check (Step 8) handles modality compatibility. Top-1 candidate PASS at platform gate this round confirms the concern is not realized in practice.
adjudications.action_taken: N/A (recurring conceptual concern; pipeline architecture intentionally exposes negative D5/D6 to enable post-hoc filtering rather than hide them in the composite)

adjudications.blocker_summary_excerpt: Profoundly negative mechanism differentiation despite high clinical/genetic signals implies complete redundancy with SoC or an historically intractable node.
adjudications.summary: Same rebuttal as ADJ-001: target-vs-comparator positioning is downstream of target-agnostic ranking per plan.md §1.2. Pipeline's job is to rank; clinical class positioning is a separate workstream.
adjudications.action_taken: N/A (recurring SoC-positioning concern; out-of-scope for AC-5 acceptance)

adjudications.blocker_summary_excerpt: Pharmacological blocker for the presumed secreted-protein/saRNA modality: secretion/modulatability is negative and mechanism differentiation is strongly negative despite strong genetic/clinical signal. Do not advance without a different modality rationale.
adjudications.summary: Same architectural rebuttal as ADJ-002 (different hash, same conceptual concern): D8 platform deliverability is excluded from composite by design + post-hoc platform check at Step 8 already filters incompatible modalities. Current platform_compatibility_top25.tsv shows candidate_001 PASS, so the hypothesized mismatch is not realized.

adjudications.blocker_summary_excerpt: Pharmacological blocker for an expression or secreted-protein modality. Despite excellent genetic, clinical, and literature scores, D5 secretion/modulatability is negative and D6 mechanism differentiation is strongly negative. Because D8 platform deliverability was excluded from the composite, this candidate may be ranked highly while being incompatible with the proposed modality.
adjudications.summary: By-design D8 exclusion + post-hoc platform check at run_pipeline.sh Step 8 already mitigates. Current platform_compatibility_top25.tsv shows candidate_001 PASS so the hypothesized modality mismatch is not realized at the post-hoc gate.
adjudications.action_taken: N/A (no methodology change; concern describes intentional pipeline architecture)
